figures.caption: Table 4: Ablation studies of FPQC-SAC. Red/blue denote best/second best.

Figure 2: Representation quality for FPQC-SAC, RFF/Fourier features, Wavelet filtering, and Kalman filtering. Lower Total Latent Variance and higher VAF@1 indicate stronger noise suppression and structural coherence.
Figure 3: Representation quality for the classical bottleneck controls in Table 1. Lower Total Latent Variance and higher VAF@1 indicate stronger noise suppression and structural coherence.

Figure 4: Extended out-of-sample median account value trajectories from 2019 to 2023. The shaded area represents the further extrapolation period beyond the original experiment. FPQC-SAC (red) maintains its relative rank and overall trajectory without structural breakdown despite the lack of retraining over the extended horizon.
Table 9: Welch’s t-test significance analysis comparing FPQC-SAC against SAC.
Table 3: Generalization experiments across defensive blue-chip and high-volatility growth portfolios. Red marks the best result and blue marks the second-best.
Table 8: Algorithmic hyperparameters for standard and quantum DRL baselines.
Table 2: Main experiment on the Mainstream Tech & Market-Index Portfolio (QQQ, SPY, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, AAPL). Red marks the best result and blue marks the second-best.

Figure 1: The system architecture and end-to-end workflow of FPQC-SAC. By forcing high-variance irregular Brownian paths through a 7-qubit, 2-layer circularly entangled unitary space, the architecture maps Euclidean noise onto a complex hypersphere. Here, isotropic noise undergoes destructive interference, naturally filtering extreme perturbations before they reach the classical Actor-Critic heads.
Table 7: Quantum Parameterized Quantum Circuit (PQC) settings.
Table 6: State space, action space, and environment configuration.
